name: Scones--Tea Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons powdered sugar (or 1 1/2 teaspoons Sweet 'n Low® sweetener)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine the measured dry ingredients with less than half the flour and butter. Blend on low speed until uniform.
2. Add the remaining flour and blend.
3. With the mixer still running on low speed, add the milk and eggs. When all the flour is moistened, increase to medium speed for two minutes. Sticky dough or batter may result; you can add currants or other dried fruits and nuts at this stage if desired.
4. Scoop onto a parchment-lined sheet pan and chill for 30 minutes or freeze.
Bake frozen scones at 325°F for 30-40 minutes, or chilled ones at 350°F for 20-25 minutes.
Or, gently roll the dough on a heavily floured board to 3/4 inch thickness and cut into 2 1/2-inch rounds. Place on parchment-lined pans and brush with egg or milk wash. These are tea biscuits perfect for classic strawberry shortcake. Occasionally, scones are shaped as wedges; flatten a 1 1/2 pound ball of dough and divide into sixths.
